text: Lesson 22
zh: 第22课

text: Knowledge and progress
zh: 知识与进步

text: In what two areas have people made no 'progress' at all?
zh: 在哪两个方面人类完全没有取得任何'进步'？

text: Why does the idea of progress loom so large in the modern world?
zh: 为什么进步的观念在现代世界里显得如此重要？

text: Surely because progress of a particular kind is actually taking place around us
zh: 无疑是因为某种特定形式的进步确实正在我们周围发生，

text: and is becoming more and more manifest.
zh: 并且变得越来越明显。

text: Although mankind has undergone no general improvement in intelligence or morality,
zh: 尽管人类在智力或道德方面并未获得普遍的提升，

text: it has made extraordinary progress in the accumulation of knowledge.
zh: 但在知识的积累方面却取得了非凡的进步。

text: began to increase as soon as the thoughts of one individual
zh: 知识的积累一旦开始便不断增长，

text: could be communicated to another by means of speech.
zh: 因为一个人的思想可以通过语言传达给另一个人。

text: With the invention of writing, a great advance was made,
zh: 随着文字的发明，知识又向前迈进了一大步，

text: for knowledge could then be not only communicated but also stored.
zh: 因为知识从此不仅能够传播，还能够被保存下来。

text: Libraries made education possible, and education in its turn added to libraries:
zh: 图书馆使教育成为可能，而教育反过来又丰富了图书馆的藏书：

text: the growth of knowledge followed a kind of compound interest law,
zh: 知识的增长遵循一种类似复利的规律，

text: which was greatly enhanced by the invention of printing.
zh: 而印刷术的发明更是极大地加速了这一进程。

text: All this was comparatively slow until, with the coming of science,
zh: 在科学出现之前，这一切进展相对缓慢，

text: the tempo was suddenly raised.
zh: 但科学一到来，速度便骤然加快。

text: Then knowledge began to be accumulated according to a systematic plan.
zh: 从那时起，知识开始按照系统的计划被积累起来。

text: The trickle became a stream: the stream has now become a torrent.
zh: 涓涓细流变成了小溪，如今这小溪又变成了洪流。

text: Moreover, as soon as new knowledge is acquired, it is now turned to practical account.
zh: 而且，如今新知识一旦被获得，便立刻被应用于实际。

text: What is called 'modern civilization'
zh: 所谓'现代文明'，

text: is not the result of a balanced development of all man's nature,
zh: 并不是人类天性全面均衡发展的结果，

text: but of accumulated knowledge applied to practical life.
zh: 而是积累的知识被应用于实际生活的结果。

text: The problem now facing humanity is:
zh: 人类现在面临的问题是：

text: What is going to be done with all this knowledge?
zh: 该如何处置这所有的知识？

text: As is so often pointed out, knowledge is a two-edged weapon
zh: 正如人们常指出的那样，知识是一把双刃剑，

text: which can be used equally for good or evil.
zh: 既可以用来行善，也可以用来作恶。

text: It is now being used indifferently for both.
zh: 如今，它被不加区分地用于这两者。

text: Could any spectacle, for instance, be more grimly whimsical
zh: 还有什么景象，比如说，

text: than that of gunners using science to shatter men's bodies
zh: 能比炮手用科学去摧残人的躯体，

text: while, close at hand, surgeons use it to restore them?
zh: 而近在咫尺的外科医生却用科学去修复它更荒诞离奇的呢？

text: We have to ask ourselves very seriously
zh: 我们必须非常严肃地问自己，

text: what will happen if this twofold use of knowledge,
zh: 如果知识的这种双重用途，

text: with its ever-increasing power, continues.
zh: 以其不断增长的力量继续下去，将会发生什么。
